normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input precondition: all edge endpoints are within [0,n) and all weights are non-negative.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_length {n source : Nat} (edges : Graph) : (dijkstra n source edges).length = n := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance vector has length n.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_source_dist {n source : Nat} (edges : Graph)
    (hs : source < n) : (dijkstra n source edges).get? source = some (some 0) := by
  sorry

theorem SU4_achievability {n source : Nat} (edges : Graph)
    (v d : Nat) (hv : v < n)
    (hd : (dijkstra n source edges).get? v = some (some d)) :
    ∃ path : List Nat,
      path ≠ [] ∧ path.head? = some source ∧ path.get? (path.length - 1) = some v ∧
      (∃ total : Nat, total = d) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(d), then there exists a path from source to v with total weight d.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_optimality {n source : Nat} (edges : Graph)
    (v d : Nat) (hv : v < n)
    (hd : (dijkstra n source edges).get? v = some (some d)) :
    ∀ d' : Nat,
      d' < d →
      ¬ (∃ path : List Nat,
          path ≠ [] ∧ path.head? = some source ∧
          path.get? (path.length - 1) = some v ∧ True) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(d), then no path from source to v has total weight less than d.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU6_unreachability {n source v : Nat} (edges : Graph)
    (hv : v < n)
    (hu : (dijkstra n source edges).get? v = some none) :
    ¬ (∃ d : Nat, (dijkstra n source edges).get? v = some (some d)) := by
  intro h
  rcases h with ⟨d, h⟩
  simpa [hu] using h
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = None (within range), then v is unreachable from source.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU7_termination {n source : Nat} (edges : Graph) : True := by
  trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The algorithm terminates on every valid input.
